stories.title: Donut Day on Pikes Peak — Family Guide for June 6-7

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Donut Day on Pikes Peak is this Saturday + Sunday (June 6-7). Ranger Talks at Crystal Reservoir, Glen Cove, and Summit. Donut Day Passport for free sticker. Reserve timed-entry now at DrivePikesPeak.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Three Pikes Peak Donut Day podcast episodes drop June 5. Each has a hidden code word — collect all three and recite the phrase to a ranger for an exclusive sticker. Donut Day: June 6-7. DrivePikesPeak.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
Donut Day on Pikes Peak June 6-7
**Confirmed facts:**
- Event dates: June 6 (Saturday) and June 7 (Sunday) [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov official event page + visitcos.com]
- Three Ranger Talk locations: Crystal Reservoir, Glen Cove, and the Summit [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Ranger Talks are at each location, covering environmental stewardship topics (preventing food litter, protecting wildlife, water source protection, Pikes Peak donut history) [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- "Donut Day Ranger Talks To Go" — 3 podcast episodes available starting Friday June 5 [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Hidden code word in each podcast episode; recite final phrase to ranger or gift shop employee for exclusive sticker while supplies last [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Donut Day Passport: collect stamps at each Ranger Talk location, earn exclusive "Leave No Crumbs" sticker by attending all three [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Donuts available for purchase at Crystal Reservoir, Glen Cove, and Summit [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Participation is free WITH paid visit to Pikes Peak (toll gate fee applies) [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Timed-entry or shuttle tickets required for June 6 or June 7 at DrivePikesPeak.com [HIGH — coloradosprings.gov]
- Gazette article mentions Donut Day alongside Get Outdoors Fest [HIGH — gazette.com]
**Source URLs:**
- coloradosprings.gov/news/donut-day-pikes-peak-americas-mountain...
- visitcos.com/events/donut-day-on-pikes-peak-americas-mountain/

stories.title: CPSC Warning — Vndueey Magnetic Men Toy Sets (Magnet Ingestion Hazard)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: SAFETY WARNING: Vndueey Magnetic Men stick figures (~$25, Amazon 2023–2025). Small magnets can be swallowed — intestinal perforation risk. Stop use, cut in half, email photo to vndueeyrecall@outlook.com for refund. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Two or more small magnets swallowed = intestinal perforation, blockage, blood poisoning. Vndueey Magnetic Men toys have 4 magnets each. Amazon 2023–2025, ~$25. CPSC says stop use. Cut in half, email photo for refund. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: CPSC Safety Warning: Vndueey Magnetic Men Toy Sets Pose Magnet Ingestion Danger — Check Your Amazon Orders

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Vndueey Magnetic Men Toy Warning

**X/Social image concept:**
Close-up of small colorful toy stick figures laid flat on a white surface — reds, oranges, blues, yellows. Studio lighting. No brand logos visible. Small red warning icon subtly visible near bottom edge. Clean white background. Lower third mostly empty for text overlay.

**Article hero concept:**
Small colorful flexible toy figures arranged in a loose cluster on a white tabletop. Medical-adjacent imagery: a pair of scissors nearby (cut hazard concept). Clean, high-contrast studio shot. Muted warm tones. No brand names. Lower portion of image open for text overlay.

---

research.facts:
Vndueey Magnetic Men Toy CPSC Warning
**Confirmed facts:**
- CPSC issued Notice of Violation (NOT a formal recall — company has not agreed) [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Product: Vndueey Magnetic Men toy sets — 10 flexible stick figures, colors: red, orange, pink, yellow, blue, bright red, bright green, transparent [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Each figure has 4 small magnets — one in each hand and foot [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Sold on Amazon Oct 2023–Aug 2025, ~$25 [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Company: Shanghai Zhidi Network Technology Co., Ltd. doing business as Vndueey [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Hazard: loose magnets; if two or more swallowed = intestinal perforations, twisting, blockage, blood poisoning, death [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Consumer action: stop use, cut all figures in half, email photo to vndueeyrecall@outlook.com for refund [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Company has NOT agreed to recall or remedy [HIGH — CPSC.gov wording]
- Note: "may have also been sold on other websites" [MEDIUM — CPSC.gov implies]
**Source URLs:**
- CPSC.gov/Recalls/2026/Vndueey-Magnetic-Men-Toy-Sets-Recalled...
- CPSC.gov/Warnings/2026/CPSC-Warns-Consumers-to-Immediately-Stop-Using...

research.facts:
D11 Free Summer Meals
**Confirmed facts:**
- District 11 running open summer food service program [HIGH — KKTV + D11.org + Gazette]
- Ages 1–18 [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Free breakfast and/or lunch [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Program runs approximately May 27 through July 31 [HIGH — KKTV]
- No sign-up, no ID, no income verification, no enrollment required [HIGH — KKTV + D11.org]
- All sites closed July 4 (Federal holiday) [HIGH — KKTV]
- Sample sites confirmed: Boys & Girls Club 1455 S. Chelton Rd June 1–July 31, breakfast 8:30–8:50 AM lunch 11:30 AM–noon [HIGH — KKTV]
- Fountain Middle School 515 N. Santa Fe Ave June 8–July 10, breakfast 7:30–8:30 AM lunch 11:30 AM–12:30 PM [HIGH — KKTV]
- D11 Food and Nutrition Services phone: 520-2924 [HIGH — D11.org]
- Full list available at D11 website or by calling [HIGH — KKTV]
- This is different from June 3 story (D49 Springs Ranch Elementary focus) [confirmed by story history]

stories.title: AAP Summer Drowning Prevention Guide — Key Rules for COS Families

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: AAP: Drowning is the top accidental death risk for kids ages 1–4. Start swim lessons after their first birthday. Four-sided pool fencing. Never leave kids alone near water. COS pools and splash pads open now. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Drowning Prevention This Summer: What the AAP Recommends for Colorado Springs Families

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
AAP Drowning Prevention

**X/Social image concept:**
A calm backyard swimming pool on a sunny day — clear blue water, a safety fence gate latch visible in the frame. No people. Calm and clear. Bright natural light. Clean lower third of image showing pool deck tile for text overlay.

**Article hero concept:**
Top-down view of a clear backyard pool with a child's life jacket and swim goggles placed neatly on a bright towel at the pool edge. No people. Clean, organized, safety-focused composition. Bright summer light. Lower third open — mostly the towel surface — for text overlay.

research.facts:
AAP Drowning Prevention Summer Guide
**Confirmed facts:**
- Drowning is a leading cause of death in children ages 1–4 [HIGH — AAP official + multiple medical sources]
- AAP updated drowning prevention guidance in 2026 [MEDIUM — HealthyChildren.org reference; described as updated guidance; single primary source]
- Key recommendations: swim lessons starting after child's first birthday [HIGH — AAP official + healthychildren.org]
- Four-sided pool fencing (isolates pool from house) is most important home safety measure [HIGH — AAP]
- Never leave children alone or in care of another child near any water (pools, bathtubs, buckets, open water) [HIGH — AAP]
- Empty water from buckets, containers immediately after use [HIGH — AAP]
- CPR training for caregivers recommended [HIGH — AAP]
- Layered prevention strategy approach (multiple layers, not just one) [HIGH — AAP]
- Life jacket use for open water [HIGH — AAP]
- Applies to all water not just pools [HIGH — AAP]
**Source URLs:**
- aap.org/en/patient-care/drowning-prevention-and-water-safety/
- healthychildren.org/English/news/Pages/Updated-Recommendations-to-Prevent-Drowning-in-Children.aspx
- contemporarypediatrics.com/view/aap-updates-drowning-prevention-guidance
